properties.displayName: VMware Workspace ONE (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: VMware Workspace ONE (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The VMware Workspace ONE connector ingests enrolled device inventory and installed application details from the Workspace ONE Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) platform into Microsoft Sentinel. It supports device compliance tracking, non-compliant endpoint detection, and unauthorized application discovery across iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS managed devices.

sampleQueries.query:
VMwareWorkspaceOneDevices
 | where ComplianceStatus == 'NonCompliant'
 | project TimeGenerated, DeviceFriendlyName, Platform, OwnerName, UserEmailAddress, ComplianceStatus, LastSeen

VMwareWorkspaceOneDevices
 | where CompromisedStatus == true
 | project TimeGenerated, DeviceFriendlyName, Platform, SerialNumber, OwnerName, UserEmailAddress

sampleQueries.description: Top installed applications across all devices
sampleQueries.query:
VMwareWorkspaceOneDeviceApps
 | summarize AppCount = count() by ApplicationName, AppVersion, BundleId
 | top 20 by AppCount desc

VMwareWorkspaceOneDeviceApps
 | where InstallStatus == 'Installed' and AssignmentStatus == 'Not Assigned'
 | project TimeGenerated, ApplicationName, BundleId, AppVersion, InstallStatus, AssignmentStatus

dataTypes.name: VMwareWorkspaceOneDevices
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
VMwareWorkspaceOneDevices
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: VMwareWorkspaceOneDeviceApps
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
VMwareWorkspaceOneDeviceApps
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read, Write, and Delete permissions are required.

instructionSteps.title: Connect VMware Workspace ONE to Microsoft Sentinel

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites

Before connecting, ensure:
1. You have an active **VMware Workspace ONE UEM** (Omnissa) tenant
2. You have administrator access to create an OAuth Client in **Groups & Settings -> Configurations -> OAuth Client Management**
3. The OAuth Client role must include: **REST API Devices Read**, **REST API MDM Devices**, **REST API Apps Read**

## Authentication Setup

Create an OAuth Client in Workspace ONE UEM:
1. Go to **Groups & Settings -> Configurations** and search for *OAuth*
2. Select **OAuth Client Management** -> click **Add**
3. Enter a Name (e.g., `MicrosoftSentinelConnector`), assign a role with device/app read permissions, set Status to **Enabled**, then click **Save**
4. **Copy the Client ID and Client Secret immediately** - they cannot be retrieved after closing the dialog

## Auth URL by Region

Select the Auth URL that matches your Workspace ONE tenant's data center region:

| Region | Auth URL |
|--------|----------|
| United States / Canada | `https://na.uemauth.vmwservices.com` |
| United Kingdom / Germany | `https://emea.uemauth.vmwservices.com` |
| Australia / APAC | `https://apac.uemauth.vmwservices.com` |
| UAT Environment | `https://uat.uemauth.vmwservices.com` |

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Configure Workspace ONE Connection
parameters.subtitle: Connect to a Workspace ONE UEM tenant

parameters.text: Provide a unique name to identify this Workspace ONE tenant connection.
The base URL of your Workspace ONE UEM console (without trailing slash).
Region-specific OAuth token service URL. Do not include /connect/token - it is appended automatically.

options.text: Yes, collect installed app inventory per device

parameters.text: App collection triggers one additional API call per enrolled device per poll cycle. For large fleets (> 5,000 devices), consider selecting 'No' to reduce API call volume.

schema.name: VMwareWorkspaceOneDeviceApps_CL
schema.description: Installed application inventory per enrolled device collected from the VMware Workspace ONE UEM API. Use to detect unauthorized, vulnerable, or shadow-IT applications across the managed device fleet.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: UTC timestamp when the record was ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: Workspace ONE device UUID (links to VMwareWorkspaceOneDevices.DeviceUuid). Injected via item enrichment from the parent step placeholder.

columns.name: ApplicationName
columns.description: Display name of the installed application.

columns.name: AppVersion
columns.description: Installed application version string.

columns.name: BundleId
columns.description: Application bundle/package identifier (e.g., com.microsoft.Office.Outlook).

columns.name: InstallStatus
columns.description: Installation status returned by Workspace ONE (e.g., Installed, Managed).

columns.name: AssignmentStatus
columns.description: UEM assignment status for the application (e.g., Assigned, Not Assigned).

columns.name: LatestUemAction
columns.description: Most recent UEM action applied to this application (e.g., Install, Remove).

columns.name: LatestUemActionTime
columns.description: Timestamp of the most recent UEM action for this application.

columns.name: SizeInBytes
columns.description: Application binary size in bytes.

schema.name: VMwareWorkspaceOneDevices_CL
schema.description: Enrolled device inventory collected from the VMware Workspace ONE UEM API. Contains hardware details, OS version, compliance status, enrollment state, and owner information for all managed endpoints.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: UTC timestamp when the record was ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: Workspace ONE internal numeric device ID.

columns.name: DeviceUuid
columns.description: Workspace ONE UUID for the device.

columns.name: SerialNumber
columns.description: Device hardware serial number.

columns.name: MacAddress
columns.description: Primary MAC address of the device.

columns.name: Imei
columns.description: IMEI number for cellular-capable devices.

columns.name: AssetNumber
columns.description: Organization-assigned asset tag.

columns.name: DeviceFriendlyName
columns.description: User-assigned friendly name for the device.

columns.name: Platform
columns.description: Device platform (Apple, Android, WindowsPC, macOS).

columns.name: Model
columns.description: Device model name (e.g., iPhone 14 Pro, Samsung Galaxy S23).

columns.name: OperatingSystem
columns.description: Operating system version string.

columns.name: PhoneNumber
columns.description: Device phone number (cellular devices only).

columns.name: EnrollmentStatus
columns.description: Enrollment state: Enrolled, Unenrolled, EnterpriseWipePending.

columns.name: ComplianceStatus
columns.description: Policy compliance state: Compliant, NonCompliant, Unknown.

columns.name: CompromisedStatus
columns.description: True if the device is detected as compromised or jailbroken.

columns.name: LastSeen
columns.description: Date and time of the device's last check-in with Workspace ONE.

columns.name: LastEnrolledOn
columns.description: Date and time of the device's initial enrollment.

columns.name: LastComplianceCheck
columns.description: Most recent compliance policy evaluation timestamp.

columns.name: OwnerName
columns.description: Workspace ONE username of the device owner.

columns.name: UserEmailAddress
columns.description: Email address of the enrolled user.

columns.name: LocationGroupName
columns.description: Organization Group (Location Group) display name.

columns.name: Ownership
columns.description: Device ownership type: C (Corporate), E (Employee), S (Shared).

columns.name: DataProtectionStatus
columns.description: Encryption / data protection policy status code.

columns.name: TotalPhysicalMemory
columns.description: Total physical memory (RAM) as reported by the API.

columns.name: AvailablePhysicalMemory
columns.description: Available physical memory (RAM) as reported by the API.

columns.name: BatteryLevel
columns.description: Battery charge percentage at last check-in.

columns.name: IsDeviceDNDEnabled
columns.description: True if Do-Not-Disturb mode is currently enabled.

columns.name: IsDeviceLocatorEnabled
columns.description: True if Find My Device / Device Locator is enabled.

columns.name: WifiSsid
columns.description: Wi-Fi SSID the device was connected to at last check-in.

columns.name: IsRoaming
columns.description: True if the device is currently in roaming mode.

instructionSteps.title: Connect VMware Workspace ONE to Microsoft Sentinel

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites

Before connecting, ensure:
1. You have an active **VMware Workspace ONE UEM** (Omnissa) tenant
2. You have administrator access to create an OAuth Client in **Groups & Settings -> Configurations -> OAuth Client Management**
3. The OAuth Client role must include: **REST API Devices Read**, **REST API MDM Devices**, **REST API Apps Read**

## Authentication Setup

Create an OAuth Client in Workspace ONE UEM:
1. Go to **Groups & Settings -> Configurations** and search for *OAuth*
2. Select **OAuth Client Management** -> click **Add**
3. Enter a Name (e.g., `MicrosoftSentinelConnector`), assign a role with device/app read permissions, set Status to **Enabled**, then click **Save**
4. **Copy the Client ID and Client Secret immediately** - they cannot be retrieved after closing the dialog

## Auth URL by Region

Select the Auth URL that matches your Workspace ONE tenant's data center region:

| Region | Auth URL |
|--------|----------|
| United States / Canada | `https://na.uemauth.vmwservices.com` |

nextSteps.stepPlaceholdersParsingKql: source | project res = parse_json(data) | project devices = res['Devices'] | mvexpand devices | project device_id = tostring(devices['Uuid'])

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The VMware Workspace ONE solution for Microsoft Sentinel enables ingestion of enrolled device inventory and installed application details from Workspace ONE UEM into Microsoft Sentinel. For more information about Workspace ONE UEM and its APIs, see the official Omnissa documentation: https://docs.omnissa.com/bundle/Workspace-ONE-UEM-API-programming-guide

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

  • Microsoft Sentinel
  • Azure Monitor Logs
  • Data Collection Rules (DCR)
  • Codeless Connector Framework (CCF)

Dependencies:

  • A valid VMware Workspace ONE UEM tenant
  • Workspace ONE API credentials
  • API access to device inventory and installed application endpoints

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 2

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
